title: henckelia tiumanica
intro: Henckelia tiumanica, also known as the "forest screw pine," is a species of flowering plant in the Gesneriaceae family, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Henckelia tiumanica include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and provide relief from pain and inflammation. Henckelia tiumanica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems.
